dataSource: Open-Meteo Marine + Forecast APIs (sourced from European met agencies)
methodology: Significant wave height (m), wave period (s), 10m wind speed (m/s). Risk band is the worst of wave/wind classifications (calm / moderate / rough / dangerous), using Douglas-style sea-state and Beaufort wind thresholds.

chokepoints.name: Strait of Hormuz
chokepoints.context: ~20% of world seaborne oil and LNG normally transits here.

chokepoints.name: Bab el-Mandeb
chokepoints.context: Red Sea entry; routes for Suez-bound traffic from the Gulf and Asia.

chokepoints.name: Suez Approaches (Port Said)
chokepoints.context: Northern entry to the Suez Canal; ~10% of seaborne crude.

chokepoints.name: English Channel (Dover Strait)
chokepoints.context: Busiest shipping lane in the world; critical for UK / NW European fuel imports.

chokepoints.name: Skagerrak
chokepoints.context: Connects North Sea to Baltic; route for Norwegian and Russian-origin crude.

chokepoints.name: Panama Canal (Caribbean approach)
chokepoints.context: Atlantic entry to Panama Canal; Pacific–Atlantic arbitrage route.

chokepoints.name: Strait of Florida
chokepoints.context: Gulf of Mexico crude exports route to Atlantic basin.
